_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Prepare legal and other relevant documents (e.g., depositions, briefs, affidavits, declarations, appeals, pleadings, discovery).
reference_document_element_description: Skill in preparing regulatory examination evidence packages, including control narratives, policy documentation, audit trails, and supporting artifacts that demonstrate compliance to examiner standards.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-010 (RC, cosine: 0.525), AB-T-010 (AB, cosine: 0.523).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.4/10 (raw 0.442). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [affidavits, appeals, briefs, declarations, depositions, discovery] vs WIDAI-only terms [artifacts, audit, compliance, control, demonstrate, documentation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.852 (P=0.856, R=0.849). Balanced P/R — the 14-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Prepare legal and other relevant documents (e.g., depositions, briefs, affidavit" and "Skill in preparing regulatory examination evidence packages, including control n" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.542 > STS 0.442 (gap +0.100). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 2.7%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [affidavits, appeals, briefs, declarations, depositions, discovery]. WIDAI terms: [artifacts, audit, compliance, control, demonstrate, documentation].
